etymology.description: 信(信) (xìn, "trust; letter") denotes the idea of trust or belief, an essential value in Confucianism. 義(義) (yì, "righteousness; justice") represents moral and ethical actions, another core Confucian concept. 區(區) (qū, "district; area") simply specifies a geographical area.
etymology.interpretation: 信(信) (xìn, "trust") implies a foundation of reliability, a value highly respected in society. 義(義) (yì, "righteousness") encompasses acting with integrity and justice, aligning with moral virtues. The combination 信義(信義) (xìnyì) signifies a place named with these esteemed principles, reflecting the cultural importance placed on trust and righteousness in the area. The term is completed by 區(區) (qū, "district"), indicating it is a specific administrative or geographical zone.

examples.chinese: 信义区(信義區)拥有许多购物中心(擁有許多購物中心)。
examples.pinyin: Xìnyì Qū yǒngyǒu xǔduō gòuwù zhōngxīn.
examples.english: Xinyi District has many shopping centers.

examples.chinese: 我们在信义区喝咖啡(我們在信義區喝咖啡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài Xìnyì Qū hē kāfēi.
examples.english: We are having coffee in Xinyi District.

examples.chinese: 每年十二月信义区都会装饰得很美(每年十二月信義區都會裝飾得很美)。
examples.pinyin: Měinián shí'èr yuè Xìnyì Qū dūhuì zhuāngshì de hěn měi.
examples.english: Every December, Xinyi District is beautifully decorated.
